OBJK-BAUTL.full_name: Business area or utility code that classifies the object by functional domain (e.g., 'ELEC' for electrical). Used for reporting across business segments.

OBJK-BEARB.full_name: User ID of the person who last processed or changed the record. Typical values are SAP usernames like 'JSMITH'. Useful for audit trails.

OBJK-DATUM.full_name: Date field (YYYYMMDD) indicating the reference date for the record, such as creation or last status change. Analysts use it for time‑based reporting.

OBJK-EAM_BADI_FLAG.full_name: Boolean flag (e.g., 'X' or blank) indicating whether a custom BADI (Business Add‑In) has been applied to the object. Useful for tracing custom logic execution.

OBJK-EQSNR.full_name: Equipment serial number used internally by SAP for unique identification when multiple serial numbers exist. Usually a numeric sequence.

OBJK-EQUNR.full_name: Equipment number assigned by SAP Plant Maintenance. Typical values are numeric strings like '00012345' and are used to track individual pieces of equipment.

OBJK-IHNUM.full_name: Technical identification number for the installation hierarchy (often a functional location). Allows grouping of equipment under a common location.

OBJK-ILOAN.full_name: Indicator flag showing whether the object is on loan or temporarily assigned (e.g., 'X' for yes, blank for no). Important for asset availability analysis.

OBJK-MANDT.full_name: Client identifier in the SAP system (e.g., 100, 200). Used to separate data belonging to different corporate entities or environments.

OBJK-MATNR.full_name: Material number linked to the object, referencing the SAP material master (e.g., '40000123'). Enables cost and inventory analysis tied to the object.

OBJK-OBJVW.full_name: Object view type that specifies the perspective of the data (e.g., 'E' for equipment view, 'F' for functional location view). Determines which attributes are relevant for the row.

OBJK-OBKNR.full_name: Primary key of the object record – a unique alphanumeric identifier for the business object (e.g., equipment, functional location). Analysts use it to join with related tables.

OBJK-OBZAE.full_name: Object status code indicating the current lifecycle phase (e.g., 'A' for active, 'I' for inactive). Helps filter objects by operational status.

OBJK-SERNR.full_name: Serial number of the equipment, often supplied by the manufacturer. Appears as a mixed alphanumeric string such as 'SN12345' and is key for warranty tracking.

OBJK-SORTF.full_name: User‑defined sort field that determines the display order of objects in SAP screens. Analysts can sort reports using this column for custom sequencing.

OBJK-TASER.full_name: Technical attribute set identifier that groups objects with similar technical characteristics. Helps in bulk maintenance planning.

OBJK-UII.full_name: Unique Item Identifier – a globally unique reference (often a GUID) for the object, used in integration scenarios and external systems.

SER03-ANZSN.full_name: Quantity posted (numeric, with unit of measure defined elsewhere). Positive values represent stock additions, negative values represent removals, depending on SHKZG.

SER03-BLART.full_name: Document type (e.g., 'WE' for goods receipt, 'WA' for goods issue, 'WR' for transfer posting). This character field indicates the business process that generated the entry.

SER03-BWART.full_name: Movement type code (e.g., 101 for goods receipt, 201 for goods issue, 301 for transfer posting). It determines the inventory impact and the posting logic applied.

SER03-CHARGE.full_name: Batch or lot number of the material, if the material is batch‑managed. This field enables traceability of specific production lots.

SER03-DATUM.full_name: Posting date of the material document in YYYYMMDD format. It is the date the inventory movement is considered effective for financial and logistical reporting.

SER03-KUNDE.full_name: Customer number linked to the movement, used mainly for sales‑related issues such as deliveries or returns.

SER03-LAGERORT.full_name: Storage location within the plant (e.g., '0010', 'A001'). It pinpoints the exact bin or area affected by the posting.

SER03-LETZNR.full_name: Sequential number of the last processed line for the given document; often used internally for error handling or re‑processing. Typically a small integer.

SER03-LIEFERANT.full_name: Vendor (supplier) number associated with the movement, if applicable. Empty for internal transfers or customer‑driven issues.

SER03-MANDT.full_name: Client identifier in the SAP system (usually a three‑digit numeric code). It separates data belonging to different corporate groups; analysts filter on this column to isolate the business unit they are reporting on.

SER03-MBLNR.full_name: Material document number that records a goods movement (receipt, issue, transfer, etc.). This numeric key is unique per posting and is the primary reference for inventory audit trails.

SER03-MJAHR.full_name: Fiscal year of the material document (four‑digit year, e.g., 2024). Together with MBLNR it uniquely identifies a posting; analysts use it for period‑based reporting.

SER03-OBKNR.full_name: Object number that links the entry to a higher‑level business object (e.g., a purchase order, sales order, or production order). It is an alphanumeric key used for cross‑referencing related documents.

SER03-SHKZG.full_name: Debit/credit indicator – 'S' for a debit (stock increase) and 'H' for a credit (stock decrease). Analysts use this to calculate net inventory changes.

SER03-UZEIT.full_name: Posting time (HHMMSS) that records the exact time of the transaction on the posting date. Useful for high‑frequency environments where multiple postings occur on the same day.

SER03-VORGANG.full_name: Transaction code or business process identifier (e.g., 'MIGO', 'MB1A'). It provides context on which SAP transaction created the entry.

SER03-WERK.full_name: Plant code where the material movement took place (four‑character alphanumeric). It defines the organizational location for inventory valuation.

SER03-ZEILE.full_name: Line‑item number within the material document. It distinguishes multiple movements recorded under the same MBLNR; typical values are sequential integers starting at 1.
